title: End Of Evangelion Reanimated or just Blu Ray
display_title: End Of Evangelion Reanimated or just Blu Ray
wikitext:
[]: End Of Evangelion Reanimated or just Blu Ray?             Quote Post by MasterRah » Tue Mar 29, 2011 2:59 pm After their finished with the Rebuild series, would you all like to see End Of Evangelion released on Blu Ray or a re-animated version put in theaters using today's animation released on blu ray along with the original End Of Eva. I'd like both. I can also see them making a whole new animated series like they did with DBZ Kai, and have it be longer than the original. I don't know. Just some ideas I had thrown around. There's still much money to be made on Eva, and I'd really like to see End Of Evangelion re-animated or at the least updated, and cleaned up for Blu-ray. What are your opinions?

[]: Re: End Of Evangelion Reanimated or just Blu Ray?             Quote Post by visionfactory » Tue Mar 29, 2011 3:24 pm MasterRah wrote: or a re-animated version put in theaters using today's animation

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Mar 29, 2011 3:29 pm Some more stuff here: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=10206

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Mar 29, 2011 4:16 pm Despite some flaws and inconsistent animation, I think that as a film , The End of Evangelion is perfect the way it is, and any "improvements" would merely tarnish it. All I want, is a nice, new domestic DVD or Blu-Ray of quality close to or the same as the recent Japanese releases. I have the Renewal DTS edition, and it is vastly better than the Manga release.

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Tue Mar 29, 2011 4:18 pm EoE definitely doesn't need to be reanimated, but it would be nice to see it cleaned up a bit or at least rereleased. My NGE Platinum set looks excellent quality-wise today, but EoE doesn't look nearly as good in comparison. I'm no expert in how animation works and how these were made, but I imagine it would be possible to release a higher quality version of EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Tue Mar 29, 2011 5:50 pm I want a completely new rescanned native 1080p bluray of it which would blow the Renewal DVD version out of the water. No redrawn animation or anything, just a new transfer from film to HD.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Tue Mar 29, 2011 6:28 pm Given how iconic it is, I think it's only a matter of time before they get around to BD release of EoE. That's all I want; reanimating it from scratch would be a waste. rhfxz1s wrote: EoE definitely doesn't need to be reanimated, but it would be nice to see it cleaned up a bit or at least rereleased. My NGE Platinum set looks excellent quality-wise today, but EoE doesn't look nearly as good in comparison. I'm no expert in how animation works and how these were made, but I imagine it would be possible to release a higher quality version of EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by MasterRah » Tue Mar 29, 2011 6:47 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Despite some flaws and inconsistent animation, I think that as a film , The End of Evangelion is perfect the way it is, and any "improvements" would merely tarnish it. All I want, is a nice, new domestic DVD or Blu-Ray of quality close to or the same as the recent Japanese releases. I have the Renewal DTS edition, and it is vastly better than the Manga release.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Tue Mar 29, 2011 6:58 pm MasterRah wrote: Is it because they paid for a lower res copy, and couldn't get the money for the actual theatrical version?

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Tue Mar 29, 2011 7:50 pm drinian wrote: The original Japanese DVD release also had the same poor-quality master. You have to remember that these were early DVDs; there were a lot of poor transfers that have since been re-released.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Tue Mar 29, 2011 8:46 pm rhfxz1s wrote: Ah, that pretty much explains what was going on then. In that case, it seems silly that they haven't done a remastered release yet. It doesn't even need to be a BD one, though I certainly wouldn't mind.

[]:             Quote Post by MasterRah » Tue Mar 29, 2011 8:47 pm drinian wrote: The original Japanese DVD release also had the same poor-quality master. You have to remember that these were early DVDs; there were a lot of poor transfers that have since been re-released. As for End of Evangelion being remade -- why don't we just wait and see what the plot of the next couple of films is? But suggesting remaking it makes about as much sense as remaking black-and-white films because they weren't shot in color.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Mar 29, 2011 8:53 pm I thought Rebuild was gonna remake the whole ending thing. Granted, it won't be EoE. But even as vastly different as it may be from the original (as Anno said it would be), it would still render the idea of reanimating EoE moot. Just friggin release EoE in Blu-Ray in the States. Which ever State-side dub company accomplishes that first will be my new best friend for life.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Mar 29, 2011 8:57 pm MasterRah wrote: Yeah, I was watching these videos on Youtube by the "V" for Vendetta guy at that Revolution forum. He mentioned how Manga Entertainment's release of EoE might be perhaps the worst anime release ever. Is it because they paid for a lower res copy, and couldn't get the money for the actual theatrical version? V mentioned how Manga paid the high price that even ADV wouldn't pay.

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Tue Mar 29, 2011 9:44 pm BrikHaus wrote: They already did that a number of years ago, it was called Renewal. The Renewal version of the TV series was released in the U.S. as Platinum. The Renewal version of the 2 movies were never released in the U.S. You can still get them online, although without English subtitles. I did so, and it was well worth the cost.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Mar 29, 2011 10:19 pm rhfxz1s wrote: Is this the Japanese Renewal then? It's a rather steep price, but I think I'd be willing to pay it for a high quality legal version of what might be my all time favorite film.[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Tue Mar 29, 2011 10:37 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I have this version. It is awesome: http://www.amazon.co.jp/FEATURE-FILMS-GENESIS-EVANGELION-COLLECTORS/dp/B0002ONYIO/

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Mar 29, 2011 10:38 pm I'll second that recommendation for the DTS Collector's Edition. Full-bitrate DTS = Win. However with the JPY/USD exchange rate being what it is, you might want to consider getting the standard "Renewal" release w/ Dolby Digital audio instead (it has identical picture quality)... http://www.amazon.co.jp/dp/B0000DJ299

[]:             Quote Post by MasterRah » Wed Mar 30, 2011 10:09 am What's the difference between the dolby digital sound, and the DTS? It sounds like it's in theaters?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Mar 30, 2011 3:24 pm rhfxz1s wrote: Is this the Japanese Renewal then? It's a rather steep price, but I think I'd be willing to pay it for a high quality legal version of what might be my all time favorite film.[/url]

I'd like both. I can also see them making a whole new animated series like they did with DBZ
